description: Upload the completed notebook and scored dataset back to Kaggle. The output is a Kaggle notebook (derived from load_tmdb.ipynb) + a Kaggle dataset (tmdb-nrc-emotional-intensity-movie-keywords) containing ~930K movies scored with NRC Emotional Intensity per keyword. Use kagglehub or the Kaggle API to push both. This is the primary deliverable of the project.
title: Publish output dataset to Kaggle

description: Add .github/workflows/publish.yml that runs on push to main: pushes the notebook kernel to Kaggle via kaggle kernels push, waits for completion, then versions the output dataset with kaggle datasets version. Enables reproducible, automated pipeline runs without manual Kaggle UI interaction.
title: GitHub Actions: automated Kaggle kernel push on main commit

close_reason: Added phrase-level keyword scoring to notebook: tokenize, multi-POS lemmatize (n/v/a), lookup in NRC intensity dict, average matched tokens, zero fallback. Added nltk to requirements.txt.
description: TMDB keywords are often phrases ('coming of age', 'post apocalypse', 'serial killer') but NRC lexicon is word-level. Direct lookup misses most keywords. Fix: tokenize each keyword into words, lemmatize tokens, look up each token in NRC intensity lexicon, average token scores per keyword, fall back to zero if no tokens match. This determines coverage quality of the entire pipeline.
title: Handle phrase-level TMDB keywords (tokenize + lemmatize + fallback)

close_reason: Renamed load_tmdb.ipynb to tmdb_emotion_intensity_pipeline.ipynb; updated notebook title cell and CLAUDE.md references.
description: User requested better names: rename load_tmdb.ipynb to something more descriptive (e.g. tmdb-nrc-emotion-intensity-pipeline.ipynb) and improve internal cell/section naming to reflect the actual pipeline purpose: TMDB keyword extraction + NRC Emotional Intensity scoring.
title: Rename notebook and improve pipeline naming

description: Pipeline should write two output files: (1) keyword_emotion_scores.csv — one row per (movie, keyword) with 8 emotion intensity scores; (2) movie_emotion_features.csv — one row per movie with aggregated emotion scores (mean across keywords). Schema: movie_id, title, anger, fear, joy, sadness, disgust, surprise, anticipation, trust. The movie feature matrix is the primary downstream ML artifact.
title: Produce dual output artifacts: keyword scores + movie feature matrix

description: Core logic: join TMDB movie keywords against NRC Emotion Intensity Lexicon (real-valued 0-1 scores for 8 emotions). Aggregate per-movie: mean/max intensity per emotion across all keywords. Output columns: movie_id, title, keywords, anger, fear, joy, sadness, disgust, surprise, anticipation, trust.
title: Build keyword→emotion intensity scoring pipeline

close_reason: Added VAD lexicon loading (load-vad-lexicon) and phrase-level keyword scoring (score-vad-keywords) reusing tokenize/lemmatize helpers. Returns NaN for unmatched keywords.
description: Integrate NRC-VAD-Lexicon.zip from saifmohammad.com alongside the intensity lexicon. VAD adds 3 continuous dimensions per word: Valence (positivity 0-1), Arousal (energy/activation 0-1), Dominance (control/power 0-1). Combined with 8 emotion intensity scores, each movie gets an 11-dimensional affective embedding. Same phrase-tokenization strategy as imdb-563 applies. Output columns: vad_valence, vad_arousal, vad_dominance.
title: Add NRC VAD (Valence-Arousal-Dominance) lexicon scoring

description: Expose pipeline build artifacts as separate versioned Kaggle datasets for reuse: (1) tmdb-keywords-cleaned — tokenized/normalized keywords; (2) tmdb-keyword-emotion-scores — per-keyword NRC intensity scores; (3) tmdb-movie-emotion-features — final movie-level feature matrix. Makes the project a reusable feature engineering stack, not just a single output.
title: Publish intermediate datasets to Kaggle

description: Move load_tmdb.ipynb to notebooks/ and rename to reflect the pipeline purpose (e.g. pipeline.ipynb or split into 01_acquire, 02_nrc, 03_score). Create src/tmdb_nrc/ package for reusable logic. Organize data/raw/ and data/external/ for NRC lexicons.
title: Restructure project layout (notebooks/, src/, data/)

close_reason: Fixed: added Accept: */* header alongside User-Agent; downloaded and verified NRC-Emotion-Intensity-Lexicon-v1.txt (9,829 rows) into data/. data/ is gitignored.
description: The notebook cell that downloads NRC-Emotion-Intensity-Lexicon.zip from saifmohammad.com returns HTTP 406 Not Acceptable. Need to either bundle the file in data/external/, add correct request headers, or find an alternative source. The NRC Word-Emotion Lexicon (binary) already works from the local file.
title: Fix NRC Emotion Intensity Lexicon download (HTTP 406)

description: End-to-end pipeline combining TMDB movie keywords with NRC Emotional Intensity Lexicon scores. Output is a Kaggle dataset of ~930K movies with per-keyword emotion intensity features (anger, fear, joy, sadness, disgust, surprise, anticipation, trust).
title: Build tmdb-nrc-emotional-intensity-movie-keywords dataset pipeline
